mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as a trend signal without requiring threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 474948M as of 2017-04-01 reflects sustained consumer spending
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth periods do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative growth periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% as of 2017-04-01 reflects tight labor market
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment deterioration signal, or Rank IC of unemployment change signal is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate exceeding 2.0% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on future corporate cash flows and increases borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.9% as of 2017-04-01 remains in accommodative zone
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC of the threshold signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth accelerates above its 12-month trend, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days through momentum effects.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.0998 (vintage) as of 2017-03-01 shows manufacturing strength
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day SPX returns is below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO growth does not produce significantly higher 5-day returns than negative growth (t-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal demand strength: when both rise simultaneously, forward-looking investors price in sustained economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires joint confirmation from both demand indicators.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1215K and RSAFS at 474948M as of 2017-03-01 and 2017-04-01 reflect broad-based demand
regime_hint: expansion phase with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint HOUST-RSAFS signal is below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or periods of simultaneous HOUST and RSAFS growth do not produce significantly higher 20-day returns than periods of divergent signals (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) decelerates below 100K per month while unemployment remains stable, signaling labor market slack emerging despite headline unemployment stability. This interaction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth concerns mount.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K while UNRATE unchanged
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146063K as of 2017-04-01 shows solid employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the interaction signal is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, creating a negative real rate that eventually triggers Fed tightening expectations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days in post-crisis recovery environments.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 244.158 and FEDFUNDS at 0.9% as of 2017-04-01
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of the inflation-rate interaction is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in late-cycle regimes.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16842.4B (vintage) as of 2017-01-01 reflects moderate growth trajectory
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods with sub-2.0% GDP growth, or Rank IC between GDP growth rate and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
